contents.value:
```markdown
# H1 - Document title
## H2 - Section
### H3 - Subsection
#### H4
##### H5
###### H6
```
**Headings** create document structure. Use one H1 per document. Leave blank lines before and after.

```markdown
*italic* or _italic_
**bold** or __bold__
***bold italic***
~~strikethrough~~
```
**Text formatting** — asterisks/underscores for emphasis, tildes for strikethrough (GFM).

```markdown
[Click here](https://example.com)
[With title](https://example.com "Title")
[Reference style][id]

[id]: https://example.com
```
**Links** — inline, reference-style, or autolinked URLs in angle brackets.

```markdown
- Unordered item
  - Nested item

1. Ordered item
2. Second item

- [ ] Task incomplete
- [x] Task complete
```
**Lists** — `-`, `*`, `+` for unordered; numbers for ordered; `[ ]`/`[x]` for tasks.

```markdown
| Name  | Age | City    |
|:------|:---:|--------:|
| Alice |  30 | NYC     |
| Bob   |  25 | London  |
```
**Tables** use pipes `|` and hyphens. `:---` left, `:---:` center, `---:` right alignment.

```markdown
> This is a quote
>
> > Nested quote
```
**Blockquotes** prefix lines with `>`. Can contain other Markdown elements.

```markdown
![Alt text](image.png)
![Logo](https://example.com/logo.png "Title")

[![Linked image](img.png)](https://example.com)
```
**Images** use `![alt](url)` syntax. Wrap in link syntax for clickable images.

```markdown
---
title: My Post
date: 2024-01-01
tags: [tutorial, guide]
draft: false
---
```
**Frontmatter** is YAML metadata between `---` delimiters. Used by static site generators.

```markdown
---
***
___
```
**Horizontal rules** create thematic breaks. Three or more hyphens, asterisks, or underscores. Blank lines before and after.

```markdown First line Second line Or use a blank line for a new paragraph. ``` **Line breaks** — Two trailing spaces create a `
`. A blank line starts a new paragraph.

```markdown
\* Not italic \*
\# Not a heading
\[Not a link\]()
\`Not code\`
```
**Escaping** — Backslash `\` prevents Markdown interpretation of special characters: `\*`, `\#`, `\[`, etc.

```markdown
https://example.com
<https://example.com>
<user@example.com>
```
**Autolinks** — GFM auto-links bare URLs. Angle brackets work in all Markdown flavors.

```markdown
Term 1
: Definition for term 1

Term 2
: Definition A
: Definition B
```
**Definition lists** (PHP Markdown Extra / some processors) use `: ` prefix for definitions.

```markdown
Here is a statement[^1].

Another claim[^note].

[^1]: This is the footnote content.
[^note]: Named footnotes work too.
```
**Footnotes** (GFM/extensions) use `[^id]` in text and `[^id]: content` at the bottom.

```markdown
The HTML specification is maintained by the W3C.

*[HTML]: Hyper Text Markup Language
*[W3C]: World Wide Web Consortium
```
**Abbreviations** (some processors) show tooltip on hover. Define with `*[ABBR]: Full Text`.

```markdown
:smile: :rocket: :tada:
:warning: :heavy_check_mark:
```
**Emoji shortcodes** (GitHub, Slack) use `:name:` syntax. Supported on GitHub, GitLab, and other platforms.

```markdown
The formula $E = mc^2$ is famous.

Display math:
$$
\sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i = x_1 + x_2 + \cdots + x_n
$$
```
**Math** — `$...$` for inline, `$$...$$` for display. Uses LaTeX syntax. Supported on GitHub and with plugins.

````markdown
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start] --> B{Decision}
    B -->|Yes| C[OK]
    B -->|No| D[Cancel]
```
````
**Mermaid** diagrams render in code blocks with `mermaid` language. Supported on GitHub, GitLab.

```markdown
<details>
<summary>Click to expand</summary>

Hidden content here.

- List items work
- Inside details blocks

</details>
```
**Collapsible sections** use HTML `<details>/<summary>` tags. Supported on GitHub and most renderers.

```markdown
> [!NOTE]
> Useful information.

> [!TIP]
> Helpful advice.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Key information.

> [!WARNING]
> Potential issues.

> [!CAUTION]
> Dangerous actions.
```
**Alerts** (GitHub) use `> [!TYPE]` syntax in blockquotes. Types: NOTE, TIP, IMPORTANT, WARNING, CAUTION.

```markdown
## My Section Title

Link to [My Section Title](#my-section-title)
```
**Anchor links** link to headings. Auto-generated IDs are lowercase, hyphen-separated. Special chars removed.

```markdown
[link text][ref1]
[another link][ref2]

[ref1]: https://example.com "Title"
[ref2]: https://example.com/other
```
**Reference links** separate URLs from text. Cleaner for repeated links. References can be anywhere in the document.

```markdown
1. First item
   - Nested unordered
   - Another nested
     1. Deeply nested ordered
2. Second item
   ```
   code in list
   ```
```
**Nested lists** indent by 2-4 spaces. Can contain any block element: paragraphs, code, quotes.

````markdown
```diff
- removed line
+ added line
  unchanged line
```

```json
{ "key": "value" }
```
````
**Language hints** after triple backticks enable syntax highlighting. `diff` shows red/green changes.

```markdown
See [the guide](./docs/guide.md)
View [the image](../images/photo.png)
[API docs](docs/api.md#authentication)
```
**Relative links** point to files in the repository. Work on GitHub and documentation sites.

```markdown
![Build](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/user/repo/ci.yml)
![Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/package)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue)
```
**Badges** use shields.io images. Show build status, version, license, coverage in README files.

```markdown
## Table of Contents

- [Introduction](#introduction)
- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [API Reference](#api-reference)
```
**Table of Contents** — Manual TOC with anchor links. Some tools auto-generate from headings.

```markdown Markdown text here.

Caption text

More markdown text. ``` **HTML** can be used directly in Markdown for features not available in Markdown syntax (centering, sizing).

```markdown
First paragraph text.

Second paragraph text.
This line continues the second paragraph.
```
**Paragraphs** are separated by blank lines. Adjacent lines without a blank line are joined into one paragraph.

```markdown
Heading Level 1
===============

Heading Level 2
---------------
```
**Setext headings** use underlines. `=` for H1, `-` for H2. ATX style (`#`) is more common and supports H1-H6.

```markdown
[link-id]: https://example.com
[image-id]: ./path/to/image.png "Optional title"
```
**Link/image definitions** are placed at the bottom of the document. Used with reference-style links and images.

```markdown
Visit https://example.com for details.

Or explicit: <https://example.com>
```
**Raw URLs** are auto-linked in GFM. Angle brackets `<url>` work in all Markdown flavors.

```markdown
H~2~O (subscript)
X^2^ (superscript)

<!-- Or HTML: -->
H<sub>2</sub>O
X<sup>2</sup>
```
**Sub/superscript** — `~text~` and `^text^` in some processors, or use HTML `<sub>`/`<sup>` tags.

```markdown
==highlighted text==

<!-- Or HTML: -->
<mark>highlighted text</mark>
```
**Highlighted text** — `==text==` in some processors (not standard GFM), or use HTML `<mark>` tag.

```markdown
Press <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>C</kbd> to copy.
<kbd>Cmd</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>P</kbd>
```
**Keyboard keys** use the HTML `<kbd>` tag. Renders with a key-cap style on GitHub and most renderers.

```markdown
<!-- GitHub supports video embedding: -->
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/xxx/video.mp4

<!-- Fallback with thumbnail link: -->
[![Video](thumbnail.png)](https://youtube.com/watch?v=id)
```
**Video** — GitHub auto-embeds uploaded videos. For external videos, use a linked thumbnail image.

```markdown
<!-- This is a comment -->
<!-- It won't render in the output -->

[//]: # (This is also a comment)
[//]: # "Another comment style"
```
**Comments** use HTML comments or the `[//]: #` hack. Hidden from rendered output.
**Task lists** (GFM) use  for unchecked and  for checked items. Interactive on GitHub.
